node.statement: FIB-RG-TAUTAU-PROB-001: For the finite Fibonacci no-mixing tau row in `summary.tex` §11.1 ([[def:RG-amp]]) + [[def:primary]] for the `h_τ = 2/5` weight underlying the `ρ^(-2/5)` scale, with beta=(j_L,j_R,u_tau s_tau) where the real scale s_tau stands for rho^(-2/5), Lean proves that the squared norm of the normalized tau-tau amplitude equals Complex.normSq(u_tau) s_tau^2 / (Complex.normSq(j_L)+Complex.normSq(j_R)+Complex.normSq(u_tau) s_tau^2), matching the displayed probability formula after substituting s_tau^2=rho^(-4/5). Lean also proves this probability is nonnegative and is at most 1 when the denominator is positive. Julia checks high-precision rho examples and WolframScript checks the exact formula and bounds. This is only finite row/probability algebra after the RG coefficients and scale factor are supplied; it does not derive coefficients, formalise real fractional powers, plot functions, or construct physical RG probabilities.
